chunk_summary: Defines treatment failure, relapse, and post-recovery monitoring for Moderate Acute Malnutrition (MAM), emphasizing structured follow-up, clinical criteria, and operational thresholds for intervention and referral.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for early treatment failure and non-response in MAM management.
Learn how to define and address relapse within 2 months post-recovery.
Explore post-recovery monitoring schedules and their alignment with service touchpoints.
key_facts: Early failure is defined as clinical deterioration or anthropometric worsening within 1–2 weeks.
Non-response is identified after 2 structured follow-up visits (2–4 weeks) with no improvement in appetite, feeding behavior, or anthropometric trajectory.
Persistent non-response by 8 weeks may require referral to a higher facility for comprehensive assessment.
Relapse is operationally defined as re-eligibility for MAM criteria within 2 months of discharge (MUAC 115–124 mm or WHZ/WLZ -3 to -2).
Post-recovery monitoring should follow a graduated schedule aligned to service touchpoints.
reference_quote: Indian facility-based malnutrition guidance defines relapse operationally as re-eligibility within about 2 months after cure. (National Health Mission, 2023)
